ai_analysis.one_sentence_summary: loadgenerator carries a default SA token it likely never uses—still fix automount for consistency, but it can trail customer-facing services if capacity is tight.
ai_analysis.true_positive_rationale: Policy conditions match: workload under test automounts default credentials despite no obvious API client behavior.
ai_analysis.attack_vector: Less interesting to external attackers, but noisy egress plus token access still helps an insider pivot during chaos testing.
ai_analysis.blast_radius: Limited to the load test Deployment; business impact mainly if generator shares credentials with prod namespaces accidentally.
ai_analysis.prioritization_rationale: MEDIUM severity but lower business criticality than storefront tiers earns a modest score discount.
recommended_actions: Turn off automount unless the harness truly drives Kubernetes API calls.
Isolate loadgenerator in a policy sandbox namespace if it must stay noisy.
Document whether this workload is non-prod-only to set stakeholder expectations.
mitigation_strategies: Ensure load tests cannot reach production namespaces via shared service accounts or routes.
